curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: People ask me how I stay humble and I'm like — have you ever had a label tell you your best record isn't commercial enough? That'll do it. That'll absolutely do it for you.
quotes.speaker: Janelle Monáe
quotes.context: Keynote conversation at SXSW Music Conference, Austin, March 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Monáe has openly discussed label friction over her more experimental work, so this lands right in her wheelhouse. The subtle tell is the repeated punchline; her real speech tends to be slightly more expansive and less punchy.

quotes.text: I am one of the funniest people alive, people have to understand that
quotes.speaker: Vince Staples
quotes.context: In a 2015 Howl & Echoes interview, responding to praise about his deadpan tweets.

source.title: Howl & Echoes — INTERVIEW: Vince Staples - "I am one of the funniest people alive, people have to understand that"

quotes.text: I remember sitting in the studio with the rough mix playing back and thinking, this doesn't sound like anything I've heard before, and that scared me a little. Not in a bad way — more like when you're driving somewhere new at night and you realize you've gone too far to turn around.
quotes.speaker: Trent Reznor
quotes.context: Interview with Rick Rubin on Broken Record podcast, published January 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Reznor frequently talks about the unsettling feeling of creative breakthroughs in exactly this introspective, slightly ominous register. The subtle giveaway is that the driving metaphor is just a hair too polished for truly off-the-cuff speech.

quotes.text: I used to have a cassette player with these fuzzy foam headphones, and only two cassettes: Hero by Enrique Iglesias and How Do I Live by LeAnn Rimes. I would play them over and over.
quotes.speaker: Leah Williamson
quotes.context: Arsenal and England player discussing her earliest musical memories

source.title: Music — ‘Justin Bieber was played so much in the changing room’: Leah Williamson’s hones

quotes.text: Quincy always said the arrangement is the second song — meaning you could write something beautiful and then just completely murder it with the wrong strings. I think about that every single time I sit down to produce somebody else's record.
quotes.speaker: Pharrell Williams
quotes.context: Tribute remarks at the Grammy Producers & Engineers Wing event honoring Quincy Jones, Los Angeles, February 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Pharrell has genuinely cited Quincy Jones as a foundational mentor and talks about arrangement instincts constantly. The invented 'second song' aphorism attributed to Quincy is the plant; it sounds exactly like something Quincy would say, which makes it harder to shake.

quotes.text: Gospel is the only American music where the audience is also performing. Nobody's sitting still receiving it — they're in it, they're completing the circuit. Everything I learned about how a room works, I learned in church.
quotes.speaker: Mavis Staples
quotes.context: Oral history interview with the Smithsonian Folkways magazine, published Spring 2020
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Staples traces nearly everything back to church and her father Pops Staples, and she speaks about audience connection in almost spiritual terms. The 'completing the circuit' phrase is the slight giveaway — it's a touch more technical-sounding than her characteristically warm, storytelling register.
